prompt:
Develop a ForgeRoom Offline Collaboration Workspace enabling small teams to run a shared whiteboard, chat, and threaded annotations in environments with no internet access such as factory intranets and trade-show floors. A Host creates a Room and acts as the session coordinator; up to 20 Participants join using a room link plus an on-screen pairing code. The core workspace is a Canvas-based whiteboard with a left Sidebar for tools and a top Toolbar for sticky notes, arrows/connectors, freehand pen, and image drop-in; each room supports up to 2,000 elements, with images limited to 5 MB each and 50 images per room. Every element can carry a threaded comment drawer (up to 200 comments per thread) and supports @mentions that resolve against the room's local people list; the chat panel retains the most recent 5,000 messages and allows pinning up to 3 messages at the top. Presence is shown via an avatar stack and cursor/name tags, and all edits generate an Activity Feed timeline (create/edit/delete, comment, pin, rollback) with filter Tabs. Joining can follow a configurable request→approval→active→leave flow: the Host can require approval for new joiners, optionally add a second Reviewer approval when the room is at 15+ people, and the UI validates state transitions (e.g., a left user cannot post) while recording who approved what and when. Roles (Host, Reviewer, Participant, Guest) are UI personas that change visible actions and menus for convenience only, not as a security boundary.

Built as a Vue.js + TypeScript SPA, the app runs fully in the browser with no backend and no third-party services. "Login" is local-only profile selection with a password-style passphrase (minimum 8 characters) used to unlock the workspace on that device; the app enforces a 30-minute inactivity lock screen and an 8-hour forced sign-out timer while the app is open, both stored as local session flags and resettable if the user clears site data. Room data, whiteboard elements, chat, comment threads, images/blobs, approvals, and the activity feed are persisted in IndexedDB; lightweight preferences (UI theme, recent rooms, last tool, avatar color) live in LocalStorage. Auto-save runs every 10 seconds, and a snapshot is created every 5 minutes while the app is open, retaining the most recent 48 snapshots with one-click rollback and a confirmation Modal. Multi-tab coordination uses BroadcastChannel to prevent silent overwrites and to show conflict Toasts. Cross-device collaboration over the same LAN uses browser-native WebRTC DataChannels with manual copy/paste (or optional camera-scanned) pairing text to avoid any signaling servers; if peer connections fail, users can still export a single backup file (≤200 MB) via Blob download and later import it, with row-level validation and error reporting, including a bulk-import cap of 1,000 sticky notes/comments per batch.
